findings.name: SQL Injection
findings.description: The product constructs all or part of an SQL command using externally-influenced input from an upstream component, but it does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes special elements that could modify the intended SQL command.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/example/photoalbum/repository/PhotoRepository.java:45 — String concatenation used to build SQL query with user input: "SELECT * FROM photos WHERE title LIKE '%" + searchTerm + "%'". This allows SQL injection attacks.

findings.name: Cross-site Scripting (XSS)
findings.description: The product does not neutralize or incorrectly neutralizes user-controllable input before it is placed in output that is used as a web page served to other users.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/example/photoalbum/controller/PhotoController.java:78 — User-provided photo description is rendered directly in HTML without escaping: model.addAttribute("description", photo.getDescription()). Template does not use th:text for safe rendering.

findings.name: Path Traversal
findings.description: The product uses external input to construct a pathname that is intended to identify a file or directory located underneath a restricted parent directory, but does not properly neutralize special elements that can cause the pathname to resolve outside of the restricted directory.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/example/photoalbum/service/FileStorageService.java:32 — User-supplied filename used directly in path construction: Paths.get(uploadDir, filename). No validation for '../' sequences.

findings.name: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
findings.description: Information written to log files can be of a sensitive nature and give valuable guidance to an attacker or expose sensitive user information.
findings.evidence: src/main/java/com/example/photoalbum/service/AuthService.java:56 — User credentials logged at DEBUG level: logger.debug("Login attempt for user: {} with password: {}", username, password). Sensitive data exposed in logs.

findings.name: Use of Obsolete Function
findings.description: The code uses deprecated or obsolete functions, suggesting the code has not been actively maintained.
findings.evidence: pom.xml:25 — Using outdated Spring Boot version 2.5.0 which has known security vulnerabilities. Recommend upgrading to latest 3.x version.
